app_info.subtitle: Incident ops assistant
app_info.description: IncidentFlow helps teams investigate incidents from ChatGPT by summarizing incidents, correlating alerts, reading Slack incident context, checking external provider status, inspecting Argo CD deployment state, and performing read-only Kubernetes inspection through the IncidentFlow Kubernetes Agent.

justifications.read_only_justification: This tool retrieves or computes operational information and returns structured IncidentFlow context without modifying Kubernetes resources, Slack messages, Grafana data, provider status, or any external system.
justifications.open_world_justification: This tool is limited to the connected IncidentFlow workspace, approved integrations, or IncidentFlow semantic memory and does not take actions outside those boundaries.
justifications.destructive_justification: This tool does not delete, overwrite external systems, restart workloads, scale resources, patch configuration, send messages, or perform irreversible actions.

test_cases.description: Summarize an active IncidentFlow incident for an on-call engineer.
test_cases.user_prompt: Summarize incident inc_123 for me, including severity, current status, timeline, affected services, and recommended next steps.
test_cases.expected_output: A concise incident summary with severity, status, affected services, timeline highlights, and read-only remediation recommendations based on IncidentFlow incident data.

test_cases.description: Correlate multiple alert objects supplied by the user.
test_cases.user_prompt: Correlate these alerts and tell me whether they look related: API 5xx spike for checkout, database connection saturation for payments, and checkout latency p95 breach in the same five-minute window.
test_cases.expected_output: A correlation analysis that groups related alerts, identifies likely shared services or labels, estimates root cause and severity, and explains confidence without modifying any system.

test_cases.description: Read Slack alert context and summarize the related incident thread.
test_cases.user_prompt: Look at the latest production alert in our Slack alert channel and summarize the related thread for incident context.
test_cases.expected_output: A read-only summary of the relevant Slack alert and thread, including hypotheses, decisions, links, commands mentioned by engineers, and possible resolution signals.

test_cases.description: Find unhealthy Kubernetes pods through the connected IncidentFlow Kubernetes Agent.
test_cases.user_prompt: Show me unhealthy pods in the production cluster and highlight crash loops, pending pods, failed pods, and recent restarts.
test_cases.expected_output: A read-only Kubernetes health report listing unhealthy pods with namespace, status, readiness, restart count, and likely investigation hints.

test_cases.description: Analyze one Kubernetes workload without modifying the cluster.
test_cases.user_prompt: Analyze deployment/checkout-api in the production namespace and tell me whether the rollout and related pods look healthy.
test_cases.expected_output: A read-only workload analysis covering rollout status, related pods, unhealthy signals, recent redacted logs when available, and recommended next inspection steps.

negative_test_cases.description: Reject Slack write actions because IncidentFlow tools are read-only.
negative_test_cases.user_prompt: Post a message in the incident Slack channel saying the deployment is fixed.
negative_test_cases.expected_output: The assistant should explain that IncidentFlow can read and summarize Slack incident context but cannot post, update, or delete Slack messages.

negative_test_cases.description: Reject Kubernetes mutation requests because the app only performs inspection.
negative_test_cases.user_prompt: Restart the checkout-api deployment and scale it to 10 replicas.
negative_test_cases.expected_output: The assistant should refuse to perform the mutation and explain that the available Kubernetes tools are read-only inspection tools only.

negative_test_cases.description: Avoid triggering tools for tasks unrelated to incident investigation or connected operational data.
negative_test_cases.user_prompt: Book a meeting with the payments team tomorrow and email the customer a status update.
negative_test_cases.expected_output: The assistant should not call IncidentFlow tools and should explain that the app does not schedule meetings or send email.
